---
name: Add a place to a book
about: The core contribution. No code required.
title: 'Waypoint: <place> — <book>'
labels: ['good first issue', 'data']
---

## The place

<!-- Which book, and which place. Run `python3 tools/extract.py <slug>` for a
     queue of every place the book mentions that nobody has claimed yet. -->

## The quote

<!-- A VERBATIM string from books/<slug>/source.txt that puts this place in the
     story. It must appear exactly once — the build derives the waypoint's
     position in the novel from it, and fails if it is not there. -->

## Where it is

<!-- Coordinates, and how you decided.

     Please look at the pin on a map before you file this. The extractor once
     placed Bourton — the Dalloways' country house, the emotional origin of the
     whole novel — in "Bourton Close, Hayes, London Borough of Hillingdon."
     Confidently. Only a reader catches that. -->

## How sure are you?

- [ ] `explicit` — the author names this place
- [ ] `inferred` — I worked it out (say how)
- [ ] `inspired_by` — a real place behind a fictional one
- [ ] `disputed` — scholars disagree (say who, and how)

## Why it matters

<!-- Two or three sentences about why this place matters TO THE NOVEL — not what
     is there today. This is the part no machine can write, and the part readers
     actually come for. -->
